dataCategories.description: Email addresses, names, profile pictures, and account credentials collected through authentication. names detected in Prisma schema fields: User.name.
Payment card information, billing addresses, and transaction history processed through payment providers.
Page views, click patterns, session recordings, device information, and IP addresses collected through analytics tools.
User prompts, conversation history, and AI-generated content processed through third-party AI services.
Email addresses and email content processed through email service providers.
Error reports, stack traces, performance data, and user context collected through monitoring tools.
email addresses, phone numbers detected in Prisma schema fields: User.email, User.phone.
password hashes detected in Prisma schema fields: User.passwordHash.

complianceNeeds.reason: Your application collects user data through 7 detected service(s). A privacy policy is legally required in most jurisdictions (GDPR, CCPA, etc.).
A Terms of Service agreement defines the rules for using your application and limits your liability.

Your application uses AI services. The EU AI Act (effective August 2026) requires transparency about AI usage, including what AI systems are used, what data is processed, and how AI-generated content is identified.
Your application uses cookies or similar tracking technologies through analytics or authentication services. GDPR and ePrivacy Directive require cookie consent and disclosure.
Your application shares personal data with third-party processors. GDPR Article 28 requires a Data Processing Agreement with each processor that handles personal data on your behalf.

Your project integrates a payment processor. While the processor handles most PCI requirements, you should confirm your integration does not inadvertently capture or log raw card data, and complete the appropriate PCI Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ).

Web applications should conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA to ensure accessibility for users with disabilities and comply with ADA, Section 508, and EU Accessibility Act requirements. Add accessibility testing tools (e.g. axe-core, pa11y, or @axe-core/react) to catch violations during development. Ensure interactive elements use ARIA attributes (aria-label, aria-describedby, role) for screen reader compatibility. Add eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y to enforce accessibility rules at lint time.
